content.claim: European Court of Human Rights rulings in Horváth and Kiss v. Hungary (2013) and Szolcsán v. Hungary (ongoing) found discrimination in segregation of Roma children and called for safeguards against misclassification; an ongoing EU Commission infringement procedure addresses systemic segregation, yet Hungary has not provided requested data on whether new diagnostic procedures have reduced misclassification of Roma students.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Hungarian education system lens
limitations: NGO allegations of culturally biased diagnostic protocols remaining in use nationwide are advocacy-sourced claims, not independently verified
No system-wide independent audit of expert panel supervision or diagnostic bias has been conducted
Implementation status of ECtHR remedies and Commission requirements is disputed and incomplete
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
